title: tragacantha canadensis
intro: Tragacantha canadensis, also known as the Canada burdock or gypsy's tea, is a biennial plant in the Asteraceae family characterized by its large, coarse leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Tragacantha canadensis include reducing inflammation and improving skin health, as well as supporting digestive health and reducing fever. Tragacantha canadensis can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the roots or leaves in hot water, or used in salves and creams to support skin health.
